claim: Each docs/gen/tools/<command>.md shall be a pure function of that tool's methodology.Tool registry entry (Command/Canon/Purpose/Status), generated wholesale by BuildToolDocs with no hand-written content.
why: A tool doc that diverges from the tool's real declaration is worse than no doc -- it misleads operators. In the Go port each tool doc is generated by generator.BuildToolDocs() (internal/generator/tooldocs.go): for every tool in methodology.Tools it renders a single document from the registry fields only -- a status-aware H1 (statusBadge: [PORTED]/[DECLARED -- not ported]), a Status section (statusLine), the Canon section token, and the Purpose line -- and writes it to domains/<domain>/docs/gen/tools/<command>.md via gen-spec (cmd/hotam/gen_spec.go). The Python-era mechanism (deriving two sections from the module docstring + argparse --help output between hand-edit sentinels) does not apply: Go has no per-tool docstring/argparse AST to harvest, and there are no inner sentinels because the whole file is regenerated wholesale on each gen-spec run (a single AUTOGENERATED banner at the top). The single-source discipline is preserved and strengthened -- the doc literally cannot diverge from the registry entry that defines the tool. TestBuildToolDocsSemanticCompleteness (internal/generator/thinkingdocs_tooldocs_test.go) verifies every methodology.Tools entry yields a non-empty doc containing its Canon, Purpose, and Command.
